segments.heText: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְיָ אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, הַזָּן אֶת הָעוֹלָם כֻּלּוֹ בְּטוּבוֹ בְּחֵן בְּחֶסֶד וּבְרַחֲמִים.
הוּא נוֹתֵן לֶחֶם לְכָל בָּשָׂר כִּי לְעוֹלָם חַסְדּוֹ. וּבְטוּבוֹ הַגָּדוֹל תָּמִיד לֹא חָסַר לָנוּ.

segments.enText: [Placeholder] The first bracha thanks Hashem for feeding all of creation — not just us, not just today, but the entire world across all of history.

[Placeholder insight] Why do we thank Hashem for food after we've already eaten? Because gratitude, by definition, comes after — it's not anticipation, it's recognition. Birkas HaMazon trains us to pause and acknowledge what we received rather than rushing to the next thing.

segments.heText: נוֹדֶה לְּךָ יְיָ אֱלֹהֵינוּ עַל שֶׁהִנְחַלְתָּ לַאֲבוֹתֵינוּ אֶרֶץ חֶמְדָּה טוֹבָה וּרְחָבָה.

segments.enText: [Placeholder] The second bracha thanks Hashem for the Land of Israel — even when we're not in it. Birkas HaMazon ties every meal to our people's story and destination.
